title: Couepia chrysocalyx
intro: Couepia chrysocalyx is a species of plant in the family Chrysobalanaceae, commonly found in the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is known for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds and infections. The bioactive constituents of Couepia chrysocalyx include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Couepia chrysocalyx is often used to prepare herbal remedies, such as tinctures and infusions, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems.
taxonomy: Couepia chrysocalyx belongs to the family Chrysobalanaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the cocoa or muskball trees. This family consists of about 60 genera and 900 species found worldwide, primarily in tropical regions. The genus Couepia is a part of this family and contains several species, including C. chrysocalyx. Couepia chrysocalyx is a tree species, and its classification is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, and class Magnoliopsida.
